name: Sqids

unions.name: DecodeError
unions.comment:
Possible Error cases when trying to decode a string to integer numbers.

If you want to show these errors to the user, you can use the [encodeErrorToString](#encodeErrorToString) helper.

unions.name: EncodeError
unions.comment:
Possible Error cases when trying to encode integer numbers.

If you want to show these errors to the user, you can use the [encodeErrorToString](#encodeErrorToString) helper.

values.name: decode
values.comment: Decodes an ID back into a list of unsigned integer numbers using the [default Context](./Sqids-Context#default).

values.name: decodeErrorToString
values.comment: Convenience function to simplify giving feedback to users

values.name: decodeWith
values.comment:
Decodes an ID back into a list of unsigned integer numbers.

The list might be empty if

  - an empty string is passed
  - the string contains a character that is not part of the alphabet

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encodes a list of unsigned integer numbers into a string using the [default Context](./Sqids-Context#default).

values.name: encodeErrorToString
values.comment: Convenience function to simplify giving feedback to users

values.name: encodeWith
values.comment: Encodes a list of unsigned integer numbers into a string
values.type: Sqids.Context.Context -> List.List Basics.Int -> Result.Result Sqids.EncodeError String.String

values.name: maxSafeInt
values.comment:
The maximum safe integer in JavaScript (and Elm) is 9007199254740991 (2^53 – 1).
See <https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number/MAX_SAFE_INTEGER>

name: Sqids.BlockList
comment:
This module contains carefully chosen words and their variations that might not be appropriate to accidentally show up in auto-generated Sqids IDs.

It uses the [official Sqids block list](https://github.com/sqids/sqids-blocklist/tree/084d15864ce0d102fd1aa0e752edb2d78e073e9f) which is also linked as [a git submodule](https://github.com/sqids/sqids-blocklist/).

You can use the [default](#default) list, or reuse only some of the [languages](#languages) and add other words that you consider undesirable.

@docs default


## Variations

Words were transformed to include these changes:
`i → 1`, `l → 1`, `o → 0`.
If the BlockList contained the word `"low"`, it would also contain the variations: `"l0w"`, `"1ow"` and `"10w"`.

_Note_: This Elm package does not calculate these variations, it only contains the block lists as strings.


# Languages

@docs de, en, es, fr, hi, it, pt

values.name: de
values.comment: German blocked words and their variations

values.name: default
values.comment:
Contains the blocked words of all languages listed below:
[English](#en), [French](#fr), [German](#de), [Hindi](#hi), [Italian](#it), [Portuguese](#pt), [Spanish](#es)

values.name: en
values.comment: English blocked words and their variations

values.name: es
values.comment: Spanish blocked words and their variations

values.name: fr
values.comment: French blocked words and their variations

values.name: hi
values.comment: Hindi blocked words and their variations

values.name: it
values.comment: Italian blocked words and their variations

values.name: pt
values.comment: Portuguese blocked words and their variations

name: Sqids.Context
comment:
The context contains the chosen base alphabet, the minimum length of all generated ids, and a list of blocked words.

@docs Context


# Creating a new context

@docs default, from, ContextBuilder

Useful for this could be the

@docs defaultAlphabet, defaultBlockList

An alternative to passing all values would be the builder pattern, where you only pass values that you want to override.


## Building a new context

You start with [new](#new), and only need to change the default values that you want.
In the end you [build](#build) the [Context](#Context), which might return an [Error](#Error).

Fore example:

    Sqids.Context.new
        |> Sqids.Context.withAlphabet "abc123"
        |> Sqids.Context.withMinLength 1
        |> Sqids.Context.withBlockList [ "block", "disallowed" ]
        |> Sqids.Context.build

@docs new, withAlphabet, withMinLength, withBlockList, build


## Context creation errors

@docs Error, errorToString


# To retrieve information from a [Context](#Context)

@docs getAlphabet, getMinLength, containsBlockedWord

unions.name: Context
unions.comment:
The context contains
the chosen [alphabet](#getAlphabet),
the [minimum length](#getMinLength) of all generated ids,
and a list of [blocked words](#containsBlockedWord).

unions.name: Error
unions.comment: Creating or building a new context might fail with an error if invalid configuration parameters are passed.

aliases.name: ContextBuilder
aliases.comment: Used to construct or build a [Context](#Context)
aliases.type: { alphabet : String.String, minLength : Basics.Int, blockList : List.List String.String }

values.name: build
values.comment: Verifies that all passed configuration options are valid, and returns either an [Error](#Error) or a valid [Context](#Context).

values.name: containsBlockedWord
values.comment:
Checks if the given string contains a word on the block list.

Note: The string is converted to lower case.

values.name: default
values.comment:
Same [Context](#Context) as created from

    Sqids.Context.from
        { alphabet = Sqids.Context.defaultAlphabet
        , minLength = 0
        , blockList = Sqids.Context.defaultBlockList
        }

or built with

    Sqids.Context.new
        |> Sqids.Context.build

but without the need to handle an [Error](#Error) case.

values.name: defaultAlphabet
values.comment: abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789

values.name: defaultBlockList
values.comment: Re-exports [Sqids.BlockList.default](./Sqids-BlockList#default) for convenience.

values.name: errorToString
values.comment: For user feedback, you can generate description texts yourself, or use these English descriptions

values.name: from
values.comment:
Construct a Context by passing all options, might return an [Error](#Error).
Alternative to using [the builder pattern](#building-a-new-context).

values.name: getAlphabet
values.comment: Returns the chosen alphabet

values.name: getMinLength
values.comment: Returns the minimum length of every generated id.

values.name: new
values.comment: Starts with the default values for alphabet, minimum length and block list.

values.name: withAlphabet
values.comment: Replaces the alphabet.

values.name: withBlockList
values.comment: Sets the list of blocked strings that must not occur in a generated id.

values.name: withMinLength
values.comment: Sets the desired minimum length of every generated id.
